description: I/O contracts for all dbSherpa node types. All datasets are pandas DataFrames passed by name through the shared RunContext.

ACTION_VALIDATOR.description: Validate LLM-selected tool and args before execution

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

AGGREGATOR_NODE.description: Merge selected values and optionally concatenate datasets

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

ALERT_TRIGGER.description: Entry point — binds alert payload to context

inputs.alert_payload: JSON object passed at workflow invocation time.

outputs.context_keys: One context key per declared alert_field, e.g. trader_id, book, alert_date, currency_pair, alert_id.

config_schema.alert_fields: Map of field_name → type (string|date|number). Non-listed payload keys in extras.standard_alert_fields are still bound when present in the alert.

constraints: Must be the first node (id=n01).
No dataset inputs or outputs.

inputs.name: alert_payload
inputs.description: JSON object passed at workflow invocation time.

outputs.name: context_keys
outputs.description: One context key per declared alert_field, e.g. trader_id, book, alert_date, currency_pair, alert_id.

params.name: alert_fields
params.description: Map of field_name → type (string|date|number). Non-listed payload keys in extras.standard_alert_fields are still bound when present in the alert.

COMMS_COLLECTOR.description: Query Oculus comms with keyword scanning

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.comms: DataFrame with columns: user, timestamp, display_post, event_type, _keyword_hit, _matched_keywords. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].
outputs.keyword_hit_count: Total keyword hit count (int). Stored as {output_name}_keyword_hits.

config_schema.keyword_categories: Optional {category: [kw1, kw2, ...]} map. When present, each row gains a _matched_categories list plus one _hit_<cat> boolean column. Combined with plain `keywords` (both are scanned).
config_schema.emit_hits_only: Also publish ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_hits"] containing only rows with at least one keyword match. Lets downstream SECTION_SUMMARY narrate just the suspicious subset.
config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

constraints: Always adds _keyword_hit (boolean) and _matched_keywords (list[str]) columns.
Scans display_post field only.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.name: comms
outputs.description: DataFrame with columns: user, timestamp, display_post, event_type, _keyword_hit, _matched_keywords. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

outputs.name: keyword_hit_count
outputs.description: Total keyword hit count (int). Stored as {output_name}_keyword_hits.

params.name: query_template
params.description: Oculus query with {context.xxx} placeholders.

params.name: keywords
params.description: Terms to scan in display_post.

params.name: keyword_categories
params.description: Optional {category: [kw1, kw2, ...]} map. When present, each row gains a _matched_categories list plus one _hit_<cat> boolean column. Combined with plain `keywords` (both are scanned).

params.name: emit_hits_only
params.description: Also publish ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_hits"] containing only rows with at least one keyword match. Lets downstream SECTION_SUMMARY narrate just the suspicious subset.

params.name: output_name
params.description: Dataset name in ctx.datasets.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

CONSOLIDATED_SUMMARY.description: LLM executive summary across all sections

inputs.sections: All section objects produced by upstream SECTION_SUMMARY nodes (context.sections).

outputs.executive_summary: Multi-paragraph executive summary. Stored as context.executive_summary.

config_schema.llm_prompt_template: Custom prompt with {section_text}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair}, {disposition}, {flag_count} placeholders, plus any vars defined under prompt_context.vars and (when mode=dataset|mixed) {dataset}. Cross-dataset refs like {executions.notional.sum} resolve inline. Falls back to the built-in template when empty.
config_schema.system_prompt: System instruction for the executive-summary LLM. Rendered with {section_text}, alert context, prompt_context vars, and context refs.
config_schema.prompt_context: Optional structured slot block: {mode: template|dataset|mixed, vars: {name: ref_expr, ...}, dataset: {ref, format, max_rows, columns}}. Same shape as SECTION_SUMMARY.prompt_context.

constraints: Default max_output_tokens: 1000.
Must run after all SECTION_SUMMARY nodes.

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: All section objects produced by upstream SECTION_SUMMARY nodes (context.sections).

outputs.name: executive_summary
outputs.description: Multi-paragraph executive summary. Stored as context.executive_summary.

params.name: llm_prompt_template
params.description: Custom prompt with {section_text}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair}, {disposition}, {flag_count} placeholders, plus any vars defined under prompt_context.vars and (when mode=dataset|mixed) {dataset}. Cross-dataset refs like {executions.notional.sum} resolve inline. Falls back to the built-in template when empty.

params.name: system_prompt
params.description: System instruction for the executive-summary LLM. Rendered with {section_text}, alert context, prompt_context vars, and context refs.

params.name: prompt_context
params.description: Optional structured slot block: {mode: template|dataset|mixed, vars: {name: ref_expr, ...}, dataset: {ref, format, max_rows, columns}}. Same shape as SECTION_SUMMARY.prompt_context.

params.name: model
params.description: Optional LLM model override.

params.name: temperature
params.description: LLM temperature.

params.name: max_output_tokens
params.description: Maximum response tokens.

DATA_HIGHLIGHTER.description: Apply colour rules to dataset rows

config_schema.rules: Array of {condition, colour, label}. `condition` is a pandas.DataFrame.eval expression evaluated against the target dataset's rows. The condition may include `{ref}` placeholders that resolve to SCALAR values via the cross-dataset ref grammar BEFORE pandas eval — e.g. `notional > {context.peak_threshold}`, `bucket == {ladder.peak_bucket.first}`. `colour` is hex #RRGGBB.

constraints: Conditions are evaluated with pandas DataFrame.eval after `{ref}` resolution.
Rules are applied in order — last matching rule wins.
Rows with no matching rule get colour #FFFFFF and empty label.
Buggy rules (unresolved refs, syntax errors, missing columns) are skipped with a warning. The run continues.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Source dataset.

params.name: output_name
params.description: Highlighted dataset name (convention: input_name + '_highlighted').

params.name: rules
params.description: Array of {condition, colour, label}. `condition` is a pandas.DataFrame.eval expression evaluated against the target dataset's rows. The condition may include `{ref}` placeholders that resolve to SCALAR values via the cross-dataset ref grammar BEFORE pandas eval — e.g. `notional > {context.peak_threshold}`, `bucket == {ladder.peak_bucket.first}`. `colour` is hex #RRGGBB.

DATA_REDUCER.description: Reduce a dataset to a bounded preview and summary for downstream LLM nodes

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

DECISION_RULE.description: Evaluate flag_count or rules → ESCALATE/REVIEW/DISMISS + severity

inputs.flag_count: Flag count from SIGNAL_CALCULATOR (read from ctx.values[{input_name}_flag_count] if the dataset isn't available).

outputs.score: Normalised severity score in [0, 1]. Stored as context.score.
outputs.flag_count: Total signal hits (int). Stored as context.flag_count.
outputs.output_branch: Branch name to route to. Stored as context.output_branch.
outputs.matched_rule: Name of the rule that fired (rule mode only; empty in threshold mode). Stored as context.matched_rule.

config_schema.rules: Optional rules list, evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins. Each rule: {name, when, severity?, disposition?}. `when` accepts `{ref}` (truthy test) or `{ref} OP literal` where OP ∈ {>=,<=,==,!=,>,<}. Refs use the cross-dataset grammar — e.g. `{executions._signal_flag.sum} >= 10`, `{ladder.symmetry.max} > 0.85`, `{context.book_count} > 1`.
config_schema.severity_map: Override severity per disposition. Defaults: {ESCALATE: HIGH, REVIEW: MEDIUM, DISMISS: LOW}.

constraints: Threshold mode (default) requires escalate_threshold >= review_threshold.
Rule mode short-circuits: first matching rule sets disposition + severity.

inputs.name: flag_count
inputs.description: Flag count from SIGNAL_CALCULATOR (read from ctx.values[{input_name}_flag_count] if the dataset isn't available).

outputs.name: score
outputs.description: Normalised severity score in [0, 1]. Stored as context.score.

outputs.name: flag_count
outputs.description: Total signal hits (int). Stored as context.flag_count.

outputs.name: output_branch
outputs.description: Branch name to route to. Stored as context.output_branch.

outputs.name: matched_rule
outputs.description: Name of the rule that fired (rule mode only; empty in threshold mode). Stored as context.matched_rule.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Signal dataset name.

params.name: escalate_threshold
params.description: flag_count >= this → ESCALATE (threshold mode).

params.name: review_threshold
params.description: flag_count >= this → REVIEW (threshold mode).

params.name: rules
params.description: Optional rules list, evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins. Each rule: {name, when, severity?, disposition?}. `when` accepts `{ref}` (truthy test) or `{ref} OP literal` where OP ∈ {>=,<=,==,!=,>,<}. Refs use the cross-dataset grammar — e.g. `{executions._signal_flag.sum} >= 10`, `{ladder.symmetry.max} > 0.85`, `{context.book_count} > 1`.

params.name: severity_map
params.description: Override severity per disposition. Defaults: {ESCALATE: HIGH, REVIEW: MEDIUM, DISMISS: LOW}.

params.name: output_branches
params.description: Map of disposition → branch_name string.

ERROR_HANDLER.description: Classify failures and select retry, fallback, abort, or continue strategy

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

EXECUTION_DATA_COLLECTOR.description: Query Solr for client orders, executions, trades, and quotes

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}
inputs.window: Optional TIME_WINDOW output (start_time, end_time) — filters rows to the window when window_key is set.

outputs.executions: Order/execution rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.
outputs.row_count: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

config_schema.source: Which Solr collection to query. Values are derived from data_sources/metadata/trades.yaml at runtime.
config_schema.query_template: Solr query; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.
config_schema.window_key: ctx.values key holding the window dict. Used when a TIME_WINDOW node is wired upstream.
config_schema.trader_filter_key: ctx.values key whose value filters trader_id when present. Empty = no trader filter.
config_schema.loop_over_books: Repeat the query for each book in the books list.
config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing. CSV columns are not re-checked against the lists below; synthetic rows are.

constraints: When source reads versioned trades/fills, trade_version:1 MUST be hard-coded in query_template — never from context.
Output DataFrame will always include trade_version=1 for hs_execution, hs_trades, and hs_orders_and_executions synthetic reads.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}

inputs.name: window
inputs.description: Optional TIME_WINDOW output (start_time, end_time) — filters rows to the window when window_key is set.

outputs.name: executions
outputs.description: Order/execution rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.

outputs.name: row_count
outputs.description: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

params.name: source
params.description: Which Solr collection to query. Values are derived from data_sources/metadata/trades.yaml at runtime.

params.name: query_template
params.description: Solr query; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.

params.name: output_name
params.description: Dataset name in ctx.datasets.

params.name: window_key
params.description: ctx.values key holding the window dict. Used when a TIME_WINDOW node is wired upstream.

params.name: trader_filter_key
params.description: ctx.values key whose value filters trader_id when present. Empty = no trader filter.

params.name: loop_over_books
params.description: Repeat the query for each book in the books list.

params.name: books
params.description: Book names when loop_over_books=true.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing. CSV columns are not re-checked against the lists below; synthetic rows are.

EXTRACT_LIST.description: Emit the unique values of a column as an ordered list — cascade primitive for fan-out keys.

config_schema.order: sort: ascending, desc: descending, first_seen: original order.

constraints: Typical use: EXTRACT_LIST(executions, 'book') → fan-out keys for GROUP_BY_BOOK or MAP.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Dataset name in ctx.datasets.

params.name: column
params.description: Column to enumerate.

params.name: output_name
params.description: ctx.values key to publish {values: [...]} under.

params.name: order
params.description: sort: ascending, desc: descending, first_seen: original order.

params.name: dropna
params.description: Exclude NaN values.

EXTRACT_SCALAR.description: Reduce a column of an upstream DataFrame to a single scalar (first, unique_single, max, min, count, sum, mean).

config_schema.reducer: How to collapse the column to a single value.
config_schema.output_name: ctx.values key to publish the scalar under.
config_schema.fail_on_ambiguous: When reducer=unique_single, raise if the column has more than one distinct value (default false — take the first).

constraints: Typical use: EXTRACT_SCALAR(orders, 'trader_id', unique_single) → feeds a downstream collector's trader_filter_key.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Dataset name in ctx.datasets.

params.name: column
params.description: Column to reduce.

params.name: reducer
params.description: How to collapse the column to a single value.

params.name: output_name
params.description: ctx.values key to publish the scalar under.

params.name: fail_on_ambiguous
params.description: When reducer=unique_single, raise if the column has more than one distinct value (default false — take the first).

FEATURE_ENGINE.description: Compose feature transforms (window, slice, pivot, agg, rolling, derive)

outputs.features: Final working DataFrame after all chained ops, published as ctx.datasets[output_name]. Ops with an `as` field also publish intermediate datasets under that name.

constraints: Ops execute in declared order; mid-pipeline `as` publishes intermediate datasets without consuming them.
apply_expr runs Python eval row-by-row — keep expressions small and side-effect-free.

outputs.name: features
outputs.description: Final working DataFrame after all chained ops, published as ctx.datasets[output_name]. Ops with an `as` field also publish intermediate datasets under that name.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Source dataset name.

params.name: output_name
params.description: Target dataset name (defaults to input_name).

params.name: ops

GROUP_BY.description: Split a dataset by column value into one DataFrame per group

outputs.keys: {values: [...]} list of distinct group keys. Stored in ctx.values under keys_output_name (default '{input_name}_keys').
outputs.groups: Each group slice is published as ctx.datasets[f"{output_prefix}_{key}"]. Conceptual bucket — runtime stores one dataset per key.

config_schema.input_name: Name of the dataset to partition.
config_schema.group_by_column: Column whose distinct values define group boundaries.
config_schema.output_prefix: Prefix for per-group dataset names. For key='BOOK_A' and prefix 'orders_by_book', the slice is published as ctx.datasets['orders_by_book_BOOK_A'].
config_schema.keys_output_name: ctx.values key for the {values: [...]} list. Defaults to '{input_name}_keys' when blank.
config_schema.dropna: Drop rows where the group_by_column is null before partitioning.

constraints: Output dataset names contain the raw key value — keep keys filesystem-safe.

outputs.name: keys
outputs.description: {values: [...]} list of distinct group keys. Stored in ctx.values under keys_output_name (default '{input_name}_keys').

outputs.name: groups
outputs.description: Each group slice is published as ctx.datasets[f"{output_prefix}_{key}"]. Conceptual bucket — runtime stores one dataset per key.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Name of the dataset to partition.

params.name: group_by_column
params.description: Column whose distinct values define group boundaries.

params.name: output_prefix
params.description: Prefix for per-group dataset names. For key='BOOK_A' and prefix 'orders_by_book', the slice is published as ctx.datasets['orders_by_book_BOOK_A'].

params.name: keys_output_name
params.description: ctx.values key for the {values: [...]} list. Defaults to '{input_name}_keys' when blank.

params.name: dropna
params.description: Drop rows where the group_by_column is null before partitioning.

params.name: order
params.description: Key order: first_seen, sort (ascending), desc.

GUARDRAIL.description: Apply deterministic safety checks to action/result state

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

config_schema.validation_key: string — ctx.values key for critic feedback
config_schema.retry_context_key: string — ctx.values key for retry history
config_schema.args: object — static tool args merged into action

MAP.description: Fan out a sub-workflow over a list of keys; aggregate results

inputs.keys: {values: [...]} list — typically from EXTRACT_LIST or GROUP_BY.

outputs.results: {results: {<key>: {<collected_name>: value, ...}}} — stored in ctx.values[output_name]. Per-iteration datasets listed in collect_datasets are ALSO published at the top level as ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_{key}_{dataset_name}"] so downstream nodes can address them directly.

config_schema.keys_key: ctx.values key holding the {values: [...]} list to iterate.
config_schema.iteration_ctx_key: ctx.values key where the current iteration's key is written for each sub-workflow run. Lets sub-workflow collectors template queries against the current group.
config_schema.dataset_prefix: Optional prefix used by an upstream GROUP_BY. When set, the per-iteration dataset ctx.datasets[f"{dataset_prefix}_{key}"] is aliased into the child ctx under iteration_dataset_alias.
config_schema.iteration_dataset_alias: Alias name for the per-iteration dataset inside the child ctx. No-op when dataset_prefix is blank.
config_schema.sub_workflow: Nested DAG: {nodes: [...], edges: [...]}. Runs once per key.
config_schema.collect_values: ctx.values keys to harvest from each iteration into results[key].
config_schema.collect_datasets: ctx.datasets names to harvest from each iteration. They become both results[key][name] and ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_{key}_{name}"].
config_schema.output_name: ctx.values key for the aggregated {results: {...}} dict.

constraints: sub_workflow is executed in topological order per iteration; child ctx is a shallow copy of parent.
Iteration-local writes do NOT leak back to the parent ctx except via collect_values / collect_datasets.

inputs.name: keys
inputs.description: {values: [...]} list — typically from EXTRACT_LIST or GROUP_BY.

outputs.name: results
outputs.description: {results: {<key>: {<collected_name>: value, ...}}} — stored in ctx.values[output_name]. Per-iteration datasets listed in collect_datasets are ALSO published at the top level as ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_{key}_{dataset_name}"] so downstream nodes can address them directly.

params.name: keys_key
params.description: ctx.values key holding the {values: [...]} list to iterate.

params.name: iteration_ctx_key
params.description: ctx.values key where the current iteration's key is written for each sub-workflow run. Lets sub-workflow collectors template queries against the current group.

params.name: dataset_prefix
params.description: Optional prefix used by an upstream GROUP_BY. When set, the per-iteration dataset ctx.datasets[f"{dataset_prefix}_{key}"] is aliased into the child ctx under iteration_dataset_alias.

params.name: iteration_dataset_alias
params.description: Alias name for the per-iteration dataset inside the child ctx. No-op when dataset_prefix is blank.

params.name: sub_workflow
params.description: Nested DAG: {nodes: [...], edges: [...]}. Runs once per key.

params.name: collect_values
params.description: ctx.values keys to harvest from each iteration into results[key].

params.name: collect_datasets
params.description: ctx.datasets names to harvest from each iteration. They become both results[key][name] and ctx.datasets[f"{output_name}_{key}_{name}"].

params.name: output_name
params.description: ctx.values key for the aggregated {results: {...}} dict.

MARKET_DATA_COLLECTOR.description: Query EBS/Mercury tick data, normalise timestamps

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.ticks: DataFrame with columns: timestamp (ISO str), symbol (str), bid, ask, mid, spread_pips, bid_size, ask_size, venue_name, seq_no. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

constraints: Normalises raw_timestamp (nanosecond int) → ISO-8601 string.
Normalises byte-string fields (raw_symbol, venue) → plain str.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.name: ticks
outputs.description: DataFrame with columns: timestamp (ISO str), symbol (str), bid, ask, mid, spread_pips, bid_size, ask_size, venue_name, seq_no. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

params.name: output_name
params.description: Dataset name in ctx.datasets.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

REPORT_OUTPUT.description: Generate Excel report with tabs & highlights

inputs.datasets: All DataFrames to include as tabs (ctx.datasets).
inputs.sections: Section narratives for the Section Summaries sheet.
inputs.context: disposition, trader_id, currency_pair etc. used on the cover page.

outputs.report_path: Absolute path to the written .xlsx file. Stored as context.report_path.

config_schema.output_path: File path for the Excel output (e.g. 'output/report.xlsx').
config_schema.tabs:
Array of tab specs. Each tab is one of:
• Static tab — {name, dataset, include_highlights?}.
  Renders the named dataset under the given sheet name.

• Expanded tab — {expand_from, as?, name, dataset, include_highlights?}.
  `expand_from` is a single `{ref}` resolving to a list, dict,
  Series, or MAP-result. One tab is emitted per item, with the
  item bound under `as` (default `item`) and substituted into both
  `name` and `dataset` templates via `.format_map`. Examples:

    expand_from: "{context.book_list}"
    as: book
    name: "Executions · {book}"
    dataset: "executions_{book}"

    expand_from: "{per_book.results}"   # MAP result dict
    as: key
    name: "Per-book {key}"
    dataset: "per_book_{key}_executions"

When `tabs` is empty, all context.datasets are included as auto-named tabs.

constraints: Tab names truncated to 31 characters (Excel limit).
Datetime columns converted to strings automatically.
List/dict cell values stringified automatically.
If include_highlights=true, uses dataset_name + '_highlighted' if it exists.
Must be the final node in the workflow.

inputs.name: datasets
inputs.description: All DataFrames to include as tabs (ctx.datasets).

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: Section narratives for the Section Summaries sheet.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: disposition, trader_id, currency_pair etc. used on the cover page.

outputs.name: report_path
outputs.description: Absolute path to the written .xlsx file. Stored as context.report_path.

params.name: output_path
params.description: File path for the Excel output (e.g. 'output/report.xlsx').

params.name: tabs
params.description:
Array of tab specs. Each tab is one of:
• Static tab — {name, dataset, include_highlights?}.
  Renders the named dataset under the given sheet name.

• Expanded tab — {expand_from, as?, name, dataset, include_highlights?}.
  `expand_from` is a single `{ref}` resolving to a list, dict,
  Series, or MAP-result. One tab is emitted per item, with the
  item bound under `as` (default `item`) and substituted into both
  `name` and `dataset` templates via `.format_map`. Examples:

    expand_from: "{context.book_list}"
    as: book
    name: "Executions · {book}"
    dataset: "executions_{book}"

    expand_from: "{per_book.results}"   # MAP result dict
    as: key
    name: "Per-book {key}"
    dataset: "per_book_{key}_executions"

When `tabs` is empty, all context.datasets are included as auto-named tabs.

inputs.context: trader_id, currency_pair, disposition consumed by the prompt template.

outputs.section: {name, stats, narrative, dataset}. Stored under context.sections[section_name].

config_schema.mode:
templated     — legacy stats-in-prompt flow. fact_pack_llm — pre-compute named facts, pass as JSON, verify each
                required fact appears in narrative (retry once).
event_narrative — format one line per row into a chronological list,
                  then stitch with an LLM.
config_schema.field_bindings: Array of {field: string, agg: 'count'|'sum'|'mean'|'nunique'|'max'|'min'}. Used in templated mode.
config_schema.facts: Array of {name, column, agg} for fact_pack_llm mode. Example: [{name: 'buy_count', column: 'side', agg: 'count_where_buy'}].
config_schema.required_facts: Fact names whose values MUST appear verbatim in the generated narrative. A retry is triggered once if any are missing.
config_schema.sort_by: Column used to order rows for event_narrative mode.
config_schema.event_template: Python format string used to render each event row in event_narrative mode. Row columns are passed as keyword args. Example: '{timestamp}  {side} {quantity} @ {limit_price}'.
config_schema.max_events: Cap events passed to the LLM in event_narrative mode.
config_schema.llm_prompt_template: Prompt with {stats}, {facts}, {events}, {section}, {disposition}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair} placeholders, plus any vars defined under prompt_context.vars and (when mode=dataset|mixed) {dataset}. In templated mode, {stats} renders a text block and {stats.<field>_<agg>} can reference a computed field_binding stat. Cross-dataset refs like {executions.notional.sum} resolve inline.
config_schema.system_prompt: System instruction for the section narrative LLM. Rendered with the same prompt_context vars and context refs as llm_prompt_template.
config_schema.prompt_context: Optional structured slot block: {mode: template|dataset|mixed, vars: {name: ref_expr, ...}, dataset: {ref, format, max_rows, columns}}. vars resolve cross-dataset refs into named slots; the serialized dataset (csv/json/markdown) is exposed as {dataset}.

constraints: Default max_output_tokens: 600.
fact_pack_llm retries at most once when required facts are missing from the narrative.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: trader_id, currency_pair, disposition consumed by the prompt template.

outputs.name: section
outputs.description: {name, stats, narrative, dataset}. Stored under context.sections[section_name].

params.name: section_name
params.description: Unique section identifier.

params.name: input_name
params.description: Source dataset.

params.name: mode
params.description:
templated     — legacy stats-in-prompt flow. fact_pack_llm — pre-compute named facts, pass as JSON, verify each
                required fact appears in narrative (retry once).
event_narrative — format one line per row into a chronological list,
                  then stitch with an LLM.

params.name: field_bindings
params.description: Array of {field: string, agg: 'count'|'sum'|'mean'|'nunique'|'max'|'min'}. Used in templated mode.

params.name: facts
params.description: Array of {name, column, agg} for fact_pack_llm mode. Example: [{name: 'buy_count', column: 'side', agg: 'count_where_buy'}].

params.name: required_facts
params.description: Fact names whose values MUST appear verbatim in the generated narrative. A retry is triggered once if any are missing.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Column used to order rows for event_narrative mode.

params.name: event_template
params.description: Python format string used to render each event row in event_narrative mode. Row columns are passed as keyword args. Example: '{timestamp}  {side} {quantity} @ {limit_price}'.

params.name: max_events
params.description: Cap events passed to the LLM in event_narrative mode.

params.name: llm_prompt_template
params.description: Prompt with {stats}, {facts}, {events}, {section}, {disposition}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair} placeholders, plus any vars defined under prompt_context.vars and (when mode=dataset|mixed) {dataset}. In templated mode, {stats} renders a text block and {stats.<field>_<agg>} can reference a computed field_binding stat. Cross-dataset refs like {executions.notional.sum} resolve inline.

params.name: system_prompt
params.description: System instruction for the section narrative LLM. Rendered with the same prompt_context vars and context refs as llm_prompt_template.

params.name: prompt_context
params.description: Optional structured slot block: {mode: template|dataset|mixed, vars: {name: ref_expr, ...}, dataset: {ref, format, max_rows, columns}}. vars resolve cross-dataset refs into named slots; the serialized dataset (csv/json/markdown) is exposed as {dataset}.

params.name: model
params.description: Optional LLM model override.

params.name: temperature
params.description: LLM temperature.

params.name: max_output_tokens
params.description: Maximum response tokens.

outputs.signals: Input DataFrame + exactly 5 signal columns: _signal_flag (bool), _signal_score (float in [0, 1] — same scale as DECISION_RULE.score), _signal_reason (str), _signal_type (str), _signal_window (str).
outputs.flag_count: Number of rows where _signal_flag == True. Stored as {output_name}_flag_count.

config_schema.script_path: Path to custom Python script (upload_script mode).
config_schema.script_content: Inline Python snippet operating on local variable `df` (upload_script mode).

constraints: ALWAYS outputs exactly these 5 columns: _signal_flag, _signal_score, _signal_reason, _signal_type, _signal_window.
Missing signal columns are auto-filled with defaults (False, 0.0, '', '', '').
Custom scripts must operate on local variable 'df' and leave result in 'df'.

outputs.name: signals
outputs.description: Input DataFrame + exactly 5 signal columns: _signal_flag (bool), _signal_score (float in [0, 1] — same scale as DECISION_RULE.score), _signal_reason (str), _signal_type (str), _signal_window (str).

outputs.name: flag_count
outputs.description: Number of rows where _signal_flag == True. Stored as {output_name}_flag_count.

params.name: mode
params.description: How the signal is computed.

params.name: signal_type
params.description: Built-in signal family (configure mode only).

params.name: input_name
params.description: Source dataset name (an upstream output_name).

params.name: output_name
params.description: Output dataset name.

params.name: params
params.description: Signal-specific parameters (overrides built-in defaults).

params.name: script_path
params.description: Path to custom Python script (upload_script mode).

params.name: script_content
params.description: Inline Python snippet operating on local variable `df` (upload_script mode).

STATE_MANAGER.description: Track retry history and iteration state

params.name: output_name
params.description: string — ctx.values key to write

TIME_WINDOW.description: Expand an event time into a [start_time, end_time] window for downstream filtering.

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in event_time_key / end_time_key.

config_schema.event_time_key: ctx.values key holding the anchor time (e.g. 'fr_start' from the alert). Required unless start_time_literal is set.
config_schema.end_time_key: ctx.values key holding the end anchor (e.g. 'fr_end'). If empty, end = start.
config_schema.start_time_literal: Literal ISO start time. Used when the window anchor isn't in ctx.values.
config_schema.pre_minutes: Subtract this many minutes from the start anchor.
config_schema.post_minutes: Add this many minutes to the end anchor.
config_schema.output_name: ctx.values key under which to publish the window dict (default 'window').

constraints: Output dict keys: start_time (ISO str), end_time (ISO str), buffer_minutes {pre, post}.
If the event time cannot be resolved, publishes an empty dict — downstream collectors treat that as no-filter.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in event_time_key / end_time_key.

params.name: event_time_key
params.description: ctx.values key holding the anchor time (e.g. 'fr_start' from the alert). Required unless start_time_literal is set.

params.name: end_time_key
params.description: ctx.values key holding the end anchor (e.g. 'fr_end'). If empty, end = start.

params.name: start_time_literal
params.description: Literal ISO start time. Used when the window anchor isn't in ctx.values.

params.name: end_time_literal
params.description: Literal ISO end time.

params.name: pre_minutes
params.description: Subtract this many minutes from the start anchor.

params.name: post_minutes
params.description: Add this many minutes to the end anchor.

params.name: output_name
params.description: ctx.values key under which to publish the window dict (default 'window').

config_schema.args: object — static args merged into action args

params.name: action_key
params.description: string — ctx.values action key

params.name: tool
params.description: string — optional static tool override

params.name: args
params.description: object — static args merged into action args

params.name: output_name
